description: Mobile Touch Controls — full touch control layer overlaid on the canvas so the game is playable on smartphones and tablets without a keyboard.

userStories.title: Left/Right movement buttons
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want two large touch areas at the bottom of the screen so that I can steer the car with my thumbs.
acceptanceCriteria: Two HTML div elements (.touch-left, .touch-right) are appended to document.body, positioned absolutely over the bottom 20% of the canvas display area (split 50/50 left-right)
touchstart and touchmove on .touch-left set keys['ArrowLeft'] = true; touchend/touchcancel set it to false
touchstart and touchmove on .touch-right set keys['ArrowRight'] = true; touchend/touchcancel set it to false
Multi-touch supported: both buttons can be held simultaneously
Buttons are visible and interactive only during playingState (hidden via display:none in other states); they are shown in playingState.onEnter and hidden in playingState.onExit
resizeCanvas() also repositions and resizes both button divs to stay aligned with the canvas
Keyboard input (ArrowLeft / ArrowRight) continues to work normally
touch-action: none and e.preventDefault() on touchstart/touchmove suppress browser scroll/zoom
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Visual feedback on button press
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want movement buttons to highlight when pressed so that I know my input was registered.
acceptanceCriteria: Each button shows a white semi-transparent background (rgba(255,255,255,0.15)) while a finger is held — achieved by adding/removing CSS class .active
Arrow icons (◄ / ►) rendered via button textContent or ::before CSS pseudo-element; 30% opacity idle, 90% opacity when .active
Highlight disappears immediately on touchend/touchcancel
No visual flicker when both buttons are pressed simultaneously
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Tap to Start and Tap to Retry
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want to tap the screen to start the game and retry after Game Over so that I don't need a keyboard.
acceptanceCriteria: A touchend listener on the canvas checks fsm.currentState: if startState, trigger the same transition as pressing Enter (fsm.transition(playingState))
If gameOverState, trigger the same action as pressing Enter (fsm.transition(playingState) + initGame())
Tap does NOT fire during playingState
On touch-capable devices ('ontouchstart' in window), the canvas text 'Press Enter to Start' is updated to also include 'or Tap' and 'Press Enter to Retry' likewise
No 300ms delay: use touchend with preventDefault
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Tappable mute icon
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want to tap the mute icon to toggle sound so that I don't need the M key.
acceptanceCriteria: A touchend listener on the canvas checks if the touch point (mapped to canvas coords via getBoundingClientRect + scale ratio) falls within a 44×44px area centered on (CANVAS_WIDTH - 28, 54)
If it does, call AudioManager.toggleMute()
Works in all FSM states (startState, playingState, gameOverState)
Icon visual updates on the next render frame (muted X mark vs sound waves)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Ranking scroll via swipe
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want to swipe the ranking list up/down on the Game Over screen so that I can see all entries.
acceptanceCriteria: On gameOverState, a touchstart listener on the canvas stores the initial Y position
On touchend, compute delta = startY - endY (canvas coords); each 30px of delta scrolls gameOverState.rankingScroll by 1 unit (swipe up = scroll down = rankingScroll++)
Scroll is clamped to valid range (0 to maxScroll)
If |delta| < 10px the gesture is treated as a tap (no scroll), preventing conflict with tap-to-retry
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Viewport meta and global touch prevention
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want the game to feel native with no page scroll, pinch-zoom, or 300ms tap delay.
acceptanceCriteria: index.html viewport meta tag updated to include user-scalable=no, maximum-scale=1.0
CSS touch-action: none applied to the canvas element and the touch button divs
touchmove listener on document.body calls e.preventDefault() when the target is the canvas or a touch button (prevents scroll while playing)
Typecheck passes (npm run build succeeds)
